body.content: **Site-404** is an incredibly small server; however, it __has already surpassed 100 members__ **in just one month** and **consistently reaches 20 to 40 players during each SSU**. Despite its size, the server features an exceptionally high-quality map set in a dense jungle environment. Its strong presentation, combined with steady community growth, has led to increasing recognition within the SCP community.

For the time being, the SCP:RP Faction Hub News Team’s Outreach Team conducted an **interview** with the **Owner of Site-404 **to gather information regarding the project’s direction, motivations, and goals. The interview questions, along with their respective answers, __are listed below__.
**1. What is your long term vision for this server, and does it differentiate from others?**

We do not have everything figured out yet. However, as of now, I plan to try to grow, not too rapidly but not too slowly. I plan to make sure our site has a proper roleplay community and that everyone has a chance here. We also plan to keep roleplay roles and staff separate, and that differentiates us from some other sites.
**2. Do you think SCP:RP is in decline or growth as of now, and why?**

I believe that SCP:RP is currently growing after an extremely long decline. I believe we are finally gonna see the sun after the storm. I have been seeing an uprise in many new servers in sandbox and roleplay. The player counts are going up! Sandbox could barely get ten-person servers, and that is slowly changing. Now roleplay servers are emerging bigger and better than ever; that is why I believe we are in a growth.
**3. ****What inspired you to create or participate significantly in the site?**

I was inspired to create this site with my friends after being fired from Jacoby. We were fired due to a disproven document and were not given our jobs back. So we made a site; we expect a max of 20 people and are now at 116 and actively growing! Site-4Ø4 was originally named Site-27 “Snow’s Point” after my former friend. So you can thank Snow and Jacoby, I suppose!
**4. ****What do you think are the hardest parts of being the owner/HC of a site?**

I believe the hardest part is to make sure your staff is doing everything they are supposed to do. As we grow, I will have to make sure staff do their job properly and actively. Which will be hard, but we will manage!
